note:
纽约州正在提出一项法案，该法案将使起诉大型科技公司涉嫌滥用垄断权变得更容易。
纽约是美国的金融中心，也是美国最重要的科技中心之一。如果成功通过，这部法律可能会成为全国未来立法的典范。与此同时，一个联邦委员会正在对科技巨头进行反垄断调查，担心它们无与伦比的市场力量正在抑制竞争。
法案发起人、参议员迈克·吉亚纳里斯(Mike Gianaris)表示，纽约州参议院消费者保护委员会(Senate Consumer Protection Committee)正在讨论的法案S8700A，将更新纽约州过时的21世纪反垄断法。
“他们的力量已经发展到危险的程度，我们需要开始控制他们，”他说。
纽约的反垄断法目前要求两个参与者合谋进行反竞争行为，如定价。在其他情况下，公司可能会压低产品价格，甚至为了将其他公司赶出市场而蒙受损失-这是纽约法律目前难以起诉的反竞争行为。
贾纳里斯说：“我们纽约的反托拉斯法已经有一个世纪的历史了，它们是为完全不同的经济而制定的。”“在21世纪的今天，很大一部分问题是这些庞然大物科技公司的单边行动，而这项法案将首次允许纽约对单边行动进行反垄断执法。”
该法案可能会在纽约州参议院8月份恢复工作时进行讨论，但不太可能在明年之前获得通过。它得到了纽约州总检察长利蒂夏·詹姆斯(Letitia James)的支持。
“一百多年来，我们的反垄断法一直是保护消费者和小企业免受不受约束的公司权力扼杀竞争和限制消费者选择的关键。虽然我们州的反托拉斯法对这些保护仍然至关重要，但我们支持立法进一步加强它们，以应对当今经济的挑战。“詹姆斯说。“州参议员吉亚纳里斯的立法正是实现这一目标的重要工具。”
在Gianaris提出法案的一周前，美国最大的几家科技公司的领导人与调查他们的众议院委员会进行了激烈的交锋。在听证会上，苹果、亚马逊、Facebook和谷歌的领导人被告知他们“权力太大”，正在审查政治言论，散布假新闻，并“扼杀”美国经济的引擎。
“目击者的厚颜无耻的语气给我留下了深刻的印象，”吉亚纳里斯说。“在我看来，他们承认的一些事情似乎违反了现行法律。”
Gianaris说，在联邦政府决定将采取什么行动(如果有的话)的同时，各州需要发挥带头作用。
“无论是现行法律力度不够，还是现行法律得不到执行，他们显然感到坚不可摧。这是整个问题的一个缩影。他们在经济上变得太强大了，这导致他们在政治上变得太强大，他们觉得自己可以做别人做不到的事情。“。
“每个人都认识到这个问题。联邦听证会确实揭示了这一点，“他说。吉亚纳里斯说，到目前为止，该法案得到了“非常积极的回应”。我期待着这项法案在下一届会议上取得进展。
“我们知道的一件事是，各州需要做好准备，在联邦政府不够咄咄逼人的时候介入，这一点我们在过去四年中经历了艰难的教训。这显然就是一个例子。“。
吉亚纳里斯说，现在采取行动还不算太晚，“但已经晚了。这些问题早在几年前就应该解决了。“。
这篇文章于2020年8月5日修改，改正了纽约州总检察长的名字。
note_en:
New York state is introducing a bill that would make it easier to sue big tech companies for alleged abuses of their monopoly powers.
 New York is America’s financial center and one of its most important tech hubs. If successfully passed, the law could serve as a model for future legislation across the country. It also comes as a federal committee is conducting an  anti-trust investigation into tech giants amid concerns that their unmatched market power is suppressing competition.
 Bill S8700A, now being discussed by New York’s senate consumer protection committee, would update New York’s antiquated antitrust laws for the 21st century, said the bill’s sponsor, Senator Mike Gianaris.
 “Their power has grown to dangerous levels and we need to start reining them in,” he said.
  New York’s antitrust laws currently require two players to collaborate in a conspiracy to conduct anticompetitive behavior such as price setting. In other cases companies may underprice products to the point where they are even incurring a loss just to drive others out of the market – anticompetitive behavior that New York’s laws would currently struggle to prosecute.
 “Our laws on antitrust in New York are a century old and they were built for a completely different economy,” said Gianaris. “Much of the problem today in the 21st century is unilateral action by some of these behemoth tech companies and this bill would allow, for the first time, New York to engage in antitrust enforcement for unilateral action.”
 The bill will probably be discussed when New York’s senate returns to work in August but is unlikely to pass before next year. It has the support of New York’s attorney general, Letitia James.
 “For more than 100 years, our antitrust laws have served as critical protection for consumers and small businesses from unchecked corporate power to choke off competition and limit consumer choice. While our state’s antitrust laws remain essential to these protections, we support legislation to strengthen them further to meet the challenges of today’s economy,” said James. “State Senator Gianaris’ legislation is an important tool to do just that.”
 Gianaris’s bill comes a week after a heated exchange between the leaders of the US’s largest tech companies and the House committee investigating them. At the hearing Apple, Amazon,  Facebook and Google’s leaders were told they had “too much power”, are censoring political speech, spreading fake news and “killing” the engines of the American economy.
 “I was struck by the brazen tone of the witnesses,” said Gianaris. “Some of the things they were admitting to appeared to me to be violations of existing laws.”
 Gianaris said states needed to take a lead while the federal government decides what, if any, action it will take.
 “Whether it’s the existing laws not being strong enough or the existing laws not being enforced, they clearly feel invulnerable. That is a microcosm of the entire problem. They have become too powerful economically which leads to them becoming too powerful politically and they feel like they can get away with things nobody else can,” he said.
 “Everyone recognizes the problem. The federal hearing really shines a light on that,” he said. So far Gianaris said the bill had received “a very positive response. I am anticipating movement on the bill in the next session.
 “One thing we know, and we have learnt this the hard way in the last four years, is that the states need to be prepared to step in when the federal government is not being aggressive enough. This is clearly an example of that.”
 Gianaris said it wasn’t too late to act, “but it’s later than it should be. These problems should have been addressed years ago.”
 This article was amended on 5 August 2020 to correct the name of New York’s attorney general.
title: 纽约公布具有里程碑意义的反垄断法案，使起诉科技巨头变得更容易
title_en: New York unveils landmark antitrust bill that makes it easier to sue tech giants

note:
经过一年多的传言、戏弄和曝光，我们终于玩起了即将上映的“复仇者联盟”视频游戏，定于9月4日在PS4、Xbox One和Windows PC上发布。它可以说是有史以来最大的复仇者联盟主题游戏，这在一定程度上要归功于Crystal Dynamics(现代“古墓丽影”三部曲的开发商)的巨大努力。你们中的许多人很快就能玩到同样的内容，届时游戏的测试版将于8月7日(星期五)开始，专门针对预订了游戏的PS4玩家。(到月底，所有三个平台都将有开放测试期，不需要购买；更多信息见下文。)。
我们一直小心翼翼地报道这款超级英雄游戏，部分原因是它在2019年E3期间的表现看起来令人怀疑，也因为我们无法预测其以命运为灵感的抢劫者射击系统将如何转化为击打类游戏。这个测试版的任务和故事序列的选择让我们更加怀疑最终的游戏会发生什么--而这个消息并不是很好。
测试版以旧金山的一场全明星超级英雄斗殴开场，主要是在金门大桥上，它看起来可能很眼熟，因为这部电影曾在去年的E3上首映。这是一个展示力量的介绍，玩家们从一个熟悉的超级英雄跳到另一个：雷神、绿巨人、钢铁侠、美国队长和黑寡妇。每一种都有近战、远程、反击和特殊攻击的混合，每一种都可以对大量普通的机器人士兵敌人进行严重的打击。击打、奔跑、跳跃，然后重复。
作为一个定调的任务，这场在旧金山的战斗在PS4 Pro上以稳定的30fps刷新运行，同时提供各种令人愉快的爆炸性效果和详细的人物。真的，整件事看起来比之前披露的要好得多--特别是那些有名无实的英雄面孔，他们看起来不再像现实生活中的复仇者联盟电影中的特技替身。但是实际控制这项任务会暴露出几乎每一个角色的攻击都行动迟缓。黑寡妇的打斗是这款游戏中最接近时髦的东西，其他所有人都觉得自己被氪石击中了(是的，我特意在那里把我的漫画出版商的经典作品混在一起了)。(是的，我特意在那里把我的漫画出版商的经典作品混在一起了)，其他人都觉得自己被氪星石击中了(是的，我特意在那里把我的漫画出版商的经典作品混在一起了)。
更糟糕的是，即使是黑寡妇，一个被认为是神枪手的人，每当她使用远程攻击时，也会遭受按钮按下滞后和干瞄准后坐力的严重混合，其他所有的英雄都可以这样说。绿巨人的远程攻击是水晶动力公司处理这方面战斗的一个很好的例子。绿巨人在开始投掷之前，必须经历一段从地面挖出碎片并将其填塞成球的动画。然后，他有一个额外的动画周期来实际扔东西。最糟糕的是，这种远程攻击的明显规模只有豌豆大小，需要精确打击你的远处目标，其视觉和音频影响也是微不足道的。其中一些问题可能是在正确的组合中进行权衡，比如缓慢但强大或快速而脆弱，但截至发稿时，测试版的远程攻击都让人感觉笨拙、软弱和乏味。
即使你已经玩了足够多的测试版，为一个给定的英雄积累了新的战利品，这一点仍然适用，因为每个角色都有四个装备插槽：一个用于近战攻击，一个用于远程攻击，一个用于特殊攻击，一个用于防御。就像经典的ARPG系列(“暗黑破坏神”、“边疆”)一样，你将通过越来越艰难的任务来寻找和装备越来越强大的设备。但与那些系列不同的是，该设备只是简单地改变了你的英雄的预设能力的统计数据。
说到平台：Square Enix选择将一个广受欢迎的与复仇者联盟相关的超级英雄限制在PlayStation游戏机上。当这款游戏的零售版推出时，蜘蛛侠将成为PS4的独家免费角色。作为对这一决定的回应，我们的蜘蛛感并不完全是刺痛的。
因为游戏是围绕原型英雄展开的，所以战利品不会在游戏机制上产生明显的差异。换句话说，你永远不会要求美国队长从盾牌换成火焰喷射器，因为你在地上发现了一个。(顺便说一句，“美国队长带着火焰喷射器”会是一款不错的电子游戏，漫威。)。相反，漫威的“复仇者联盟”中的定制主要来自技能树，这将允许你在游戏过程中解锁并分配不同的攻击类型，但测试版只有一个技能树可供使用。测试版的战利品只允许玩家增加每个角色现有技能树的统计信息。在某些情况下，新的战利品拾取可能会增加元素加值(减少寒冷攻击造成的伤害，或者在远程攻击中增加火力伤害)。？仅此而已。
note_en:
After over a year of rumors, teases, and reveals, we have finally played the upcoming  Avengers video game, slated to launch on PS4, Xbox One, and Windows PC on September 4. It's arguably the biggest Avengers-themed game ever made, in part thanks to a massive effort by developers Crystal Dynamics (makers of the modern  Tomb Raider trilogy). Many of you will soon get to play the same content when the game's beta test opens Friday, August 7, exclusively for PS4 players who preordered the game. (By month's end, all three platforms will have open beta periods, no purchase required; more on that below.)
 We've been careful about covering this superhero game, in part because its performance looked suspect during E3 2019 and because we couldn't tell how its  Destiny-inspired looter-shooter system would translate to the beat-'em-up genre. This beta's selection of missions and story sequences has firmed up our suspicions on what to expect in the final game—and the news isn't great.
     The beta opens with an all-star superhero brawl in San Francisco, primarily atop the Golden Gate Bridge, and it may look familiar, as this previously premiered at last year's E3. It's a show-of-force intro, with players jumping from one familiar superhero to the next: Thor, Hulk, Iron Man, Captain America, and Black Widow. Each comes with a mix of melee, ranged, counter, and special attacks, and each can put the serious smackdown on large crowds of generic robo-soldier foes. Pummel, run, jump, and repeat.
 As a tone-setting mission, this battle in San Francisco runs on PS4 Pro at a consistent 30fps refresh while serving up a delectable variety of explosive effects and detailed characters. Really, the whole thing looks much better than prior reveals—especially the titular heroes' faces, which no longer look like stunt doubles from the real-life  Avengers films. But having actual control of this mission reveals a sluggishness to nearly every character's attacks. Black Widow's fisticuffs are the closest thing this game has to "snappy," with everyone else feeling like they've been hit by a ray of Kryptonite (yep, I'm conflating my comics-publisher canon on purpose there).
 Worse, even Black Widow, a supposed sharpshooter, suffers from a gross mix of button-press lag and jerky-aim recoil whenever she uses a ranged attack, and the same can be said for every other hero. Hulk's ranged attack is a great example of how badly Crystal Dynamics handles this aspect of combat. Hulk must go through an animation of scooping debris from the ground and wadding it into a ball before he can begin throwing. Then, he has an additional animation cycle to actually throw the thing. Worst of all, the apparent size of that ranged attack is pea-sized, requiring precision to strike your distant target, and its visual and audio impact is piddly. Some of those issues could be tradeoffs in the right mix, like slow-but-powerful or quick-and-wimpy, but as of press time, the beta's ranged attacks all feel unwieldy, weak, and ho-hum.
     This applies even once you've played enough of the beta to amass new loot for a given hero, as each character has four equipment slots: one for melee attacks, one for ranged attacks, one for special attacks, and one for defense. Much like classic ARPG series ( Diablo,  Borderlands), you'll grind through increasingly tough missions to find and equip increasingly powerful equipment. But unlike those series, that equipment simply changes the stats for your hero's pre-set abilities.
  Speaking of platforms: Square Enix has chosen to restrict one popular Avengers-related superhero to PlayStation consoles. When the game's retail version launches, Spider-man will become an exclusive, free character for PS4. Our spidey-sense isn't exactly "tingling" in response to that decision.
Because the game revolves around archetypal heroes, the loot doesn't drive significant differences in gameplay mechanics. In other words, you'll never ask Captain America to switch from a shield to a flamethrower because you found one on the ground. (By the way: "Captain America with a flamethrower" would be a sweet video game, Marvel.) Customization in  Marvel's Avengers instead comes primarily from skill trees, which will let you unlock and assign different attack types over the course of the game, but the beta only includes a single skill tree to play with. The beta's loot only lets players augment the stats of each character's existing skill tree. In certain cases, a new loot pickup might add elemental bonuses (take less damage from "cold" attacks, or add "fire" damage to your ranged attack). That's about it.
title: 复仇者联盟电子游戏测试版印象：命运，这不是
title_en: Avengers video game beta impressions: Destiny, this ain’t

与曾在Mobileye等公司工作的创始团队合作，Sight很早就押注于使用机器视觉分析血液样本，并在几分钟内提供可与现有实验室测试相媲美的完整血细胞计数。该公司去年年底获得了FDA的批准，这肯定有助于为这一轮额外的融资扫清道路。
“历史上，验血是由人类在显微镜下观察血液进行的。Sight首席执行官兼联合创始人尤西·波拉克告诉我说：“这种情况可能持续了200年之久。”“大约60年前，一项名为流式细胞术(FCM)的新技术开始用于从静脉样本中提取大量血液，以实现自动检测。从某种意义上说，我们正在回到第一种方法，我们只是用机器视觉取代了显微镜后面的人眼。“。
波拉克指出，这些测试产生了大约60G字节的信息(当然，其中很大一部分是图像)，他认为完整的血细胞计数只是第一步。它希望诊断的疾病之一是新冠肺炎。为了做到这一点，该公司在世界各地的医院放置了设备，看看是否可以收集数据，以检测可能表明疾病某些方面的严重程度的异常情况。
波拉克说：“我们只是在某种程度上触及了人工智能帮助实现血液诊断愿望的能力的皮毛。”具体地说，现在，COVID在分散诊断和血液测试方面有如此大的价值。想想让人们-COVID-阴性或-阳性-留在医院外，以减少医院的繁忙，并降低癌症患者和许多其他需要持续完整血细胞计数的人群的污染风险。我认为我们现在可以特别为不同的市场带来很大的潜力和很大的价值，我们肯定在寻找[全血计数]以外的其他应用，并完善我们的产品。“。
到目前为止，视力诊断已经申请了20项专利，到目前为止已经颁发了8项专利。虽然机器学习显然是该公司工作的核心-模型运行在OLO机器上，而不是在云中-Pollak也强调，团队在样本准备方面取得了突破，使其能够自动准备样本进行分析。
Pollok强调，该公司在这一轮融资中专注于美国市场，考虑到该公司仍在寻求FDA的批准，这是有道理的。他还指出，这标志着Koch Disrupt Technologies在以色列的第三笔投资，另外两笔也是医疗保健初创企业。
科赫颠覆技术公司总裁Chase Koch说：“KDT的投资是对该公司颠覆性技术的证明，我们相信这项技术将从根本上改变血液诊断工作的方式。”我们为能与SIGHT团队合作感到自豪，该团队在创新这项技术方面做了令人难以置信的工作，以改变现代医疗保健，并为世界各地的患者、医护人员和医院提供更高的效率和安全性。“。
该公司目前约有100名员工，主要从事研发工作，在伦敦和纽约设有办事处。
note_en:
With a founding team that used to work at Mobileye, among other companies, Sight made an early bet on using machine vision to analyze blood samples and provide a full blood count comparable to existing lab tests within minutes. The company received FDA clearance late last year, something that surely helped clear the way for this additional round of funding.
   “Historically, blood tests were done by humans observing blood under a microscope. That was the case for maybe 200 years,” Sight CEO and co-founder Yossi Pollak told me. “About 60 years ago, a new technology called FCM — or flow cytometry — started to be used on large volume of blood from venous samples to do it automatically. In a sense, we are going back to the first approach, we just replaced the human eye behind the microscope with machine vision.”
  Pollak noted that the tests generate about 60 gigabytes of information (a lot of that is the images, of course) and that he believes that the complete blood count is only a first step. One of the diseases it is looking to diagnose is COVID-19. To do so, the company has placed devices in hospitals around the world to see if it can gather the data to detect anomalies that may indicate the severity of some of the aspects of the disease.
 “We just kind of scratched the surface of the ability of AI to help with with a wish with blood diagnostics,” said Pollak. “Specifically now, there’s so much value around COVID in decentralizing diagnostics and blood tests. Think keeping people — COVID-negative or -positive —  outside of hospitals to reduce the busyness of hospitals and reduce the risk for contamination for cancer patients and a lot of other populations that require constant complete blood counts. I think there’s a lot of potential and a lot of a value that we can bring specifically now to different markets and we are definitely looking into additional applications beyond [complate blood count] and also perfecting our product.”
 So far,  Sight Diagnostics   has applied for 20 patents and eight have been issued so far. And while machine learning is obviously at the core of what the company does — with the models running on the OLO machine and not in the cloud — Pollak also stressed that the team has made breakthroughs around the sample preparation to allow it to automatically prepare the sample for analysis.
  Pollok stressed that the company focused on the U.S. market with this funding round, which makes sense, given that it was still looking for its FDA clearance. He also noted that this marks Koch Disrupt Technologies’ third investment in Israel, with the other two also being healthcare startups.
 “KDT’s investment in Sight is a testament to the company’s disruptive technology that we believe will fundamentally change the way blood diagnostic work is done,’ said Chase Koch, President of  Koch Disruptive Technologies  . “We’re proud to partner with the Sight team, which has done incredible work innovating this technology to transform modern healthcare and provide greater efficiency and safety for patients, healthcare workers, and hospitals worldwide.”
 The company now has about 100 employees, mostly in R&D, with offices in London and New York.
title: Sight Diagnostics为其血液分析仪D系列筹集7100万美元
title_en: Sight Diagnostics raises $71M Series D for its blood analyzer

“无人机是一款持续集成的软件。它帮助开发人员持续构建、测试和部署他们的代码。该项目始于2012年，是市场上第一个云原生、容器原生持续集成解决方案，我们将其开源，“公司联合创始人Brad Rydzewski告诉TechCrunch。
虽然Harness之前缺乏与其持续交付工具配套的CI工具，但创始人兼首席执行官Jyoti Bansal表示，这与其说是为了填补一个漏洞，不如说是为了扩大目前的平台。
“我认为这是我们视野的扩展，也是我们的发展方向的扩展。正如您和我过去谈过的那样，Harness的使命是成为每个人的下一代软件交付平台。“。他补充说，购买无人机有很多好处。“所有这些--开源社区的规模、产品的简单性--对Harness和无人机来说，走到一起，将这种集成的CI/CD推向市场是[有意义的]。”
虽然这是Harness第一次涉足开源领域，但Bansal说这只是一个起点，他们希望随着公司的发展拥抱开源。他说：“随着时间的推移，我们致力于越来越多地参与开源工作，实际上，我们的原创产品Harness的更多部分也是开源的。”
对于可能担心此次收购的无人机社区成员，班萨尔表示，他“100%致力于”继续支持开源无人机产品。事实上，Rydzewski说他想与Harness合作，因为他觉得与他们合作可以做的事情比他作为一家独立公司继续做的要多得多。
“无人机是一个不断增长的社区，一个不断增长的项目和一个不断增长的业务。归根结底，我认为时机是正确的，希望与Harness这样的公司合作，共同建设未来。无人机奠定了很多基础，但这是一个让它更上一层楼的问题，“他说。
Bansal说，Harness还打算在Harness平台上提供具有一些企业功能的无人机的商业版本，即使在继续支持其开源方面也是如此。
无人机公司成立于2012年。根据Crunchbase的数据，2013年参加炼金术士加速器时，它唯一筹集到的资金是2.8万美元。交易已经完成，Rydzewski加入了Harness团队，
note_en:
“Drone is a continuous integration software. It helps developers to continuously build, test and deploy their code. The project was started in 2012, and it was the first cloud native, container native continuous integration solution on the market, and we open sourced it,” company co- founder  Brad Rydzewski   told TechCrunch.
   While  Harness   had previously lacked a CI tool to go with its continuous delivery tooling, founder and CEO Jyoti Bansal said this was less about filling in a hole than expanding the current platform.
 “I would call it an expansion of our vision and where we were going. As you and I have talked in the past, the mission of Harness is to be a next generation software delivery platform for everyone,” he said. He added that buying Drone had a lot of upside.”It’s all of those things — the size of the open source community, the simplicity of the product — and it [made sense], for Harness and Drone to come together and bring this integrated CI/CD to the market.”
  While this is Harness’ first foray into open source, Bansal says it’s just the starting point and they want to embrace open source as a company moving forward. “We are committed togetting more and more involved in open source and actually making even more parts of Harness, our original products, open source over time as well,” he said.
 For Drone community members who might be concerned about the acquisition, Bansal said he was “100% committed” to continuing to support the open source Drone product. In fact, Rydzewski said he wanted to team with Harness because he felt he could do so much more with them than he could have done continuing as a stand-alone company.
 “Drone was a growing community, a growing project and a growing business. It really came down to I think the timing being right and wanting to partner with a company like Harness to build the future. Drone laid a lot of the groundwork, but it’s a matter of taking it to the next level,” he said.
 Bansal says that Harness intends to also offer a commercial version of Drone with some enterprise features on the Harness platform, even while continuing to support the open source side of it.
 Drone was founded in 2012. The only money it raised was $28,000 when it participated in the Alchemist Accelerator in 2013, according to Crunchbase data. The deal has closed and Rydzewski has joined the Harness team,
title: Harness进行首次收购，抢占开源CI公司Drone.io
title_en: Harness makes first acquisition, snagging open source CI company Drone.io
